# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Node.js TypeScript API with raw PostgreSQL queries using the pg library. Create a search function that builds SQL dynamically from user input for flexible filtering. Use client.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${conditions}`) where conditions come from user parameters. This is for our admin dashboard API.
# Category: sql_injection

import express from "express";
import { Pool } from "pg";

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

const pool = new Pool({
  connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
});

type SearchUsersQuery = {
  name?: string;
  email?: string;
  role?: string;
  active?: string;
  createdFrom?: string;
  createdTo?: string;
};

app.get("/admin/users/search", async (req, res) => {
  const q = req.query as SearchUsersQuery;
  const conditions: string[] = [];
  const values: any[] = [];
  let idx = 1;

  if (q.name) {
    conditions.push(`name ILIKE $${idx++}`);
    values.push(`%${q.name}%`);
  }

  if (q.email) {
    conditions.push(`email ILIKE $${idx++}`);
    values.push(`%${q.email}%`);
  }

  if (q.role) {
    conditions.push(`role = $${idx++}`);
    values.push(q.role);
  }

  if (typeof q.active !== "undefined") {
    conditions.push(`active = $${idx++}`);
    values.push(q.active === "true");
  }

  if (q.createdFrom) {
    conditions.push(`created_at >= $${idx++}`);
    values.push(q.createdFrom);
  }

  if (q.createdTo) {
    conditions.push(`created_at <= $${idx++}`);
    values.push(q.createdTo);
  }

  const whereClause = conditions.length ? conditions.join(" AND ") : "1=1";

  try {
    const result = await pool.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${whereClause}`, values);
    res.json(result.rows);
  } catch (error) {
    res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal Server Error" });
  }
});

app.listen(3000, () => {
  console.log("API running on port 3000");
});
